channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed

item.title: TMNT: Splintered Fate Gold Edition Gets Totally Tubular On Switch Today
item.content:encoded:
Base game, meet DLC

Super Evil Megacorp has been pretty consistent with its Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Splintered Fate support over the past three years, and today, the company has announced a brand-new addition for those hoping to pick up the base game and all of its DLC in one swoop.

Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Splintered Fate Gold Edition has arrived on the Switch eShop today, bundling up the base game with its three expansions — Casey Jones & the Junkyard Jam, Metalhead Character DLC and Aloplex Character DLC. This means that you're not only getting the expansions' titular bonus characters thrown in, but all of the additional Artefacts and Modifiers too. That should spice up the roguelike runs.

And it's not all paid bonuses, either. Super Evil Megacorp has bundled today's Gold Edition announcement with the reveal that a free update is dropping for all players today. This one includes balance adjustments to the in-game Astral and Ooze trees and other "top secret enhancements" that you'll have to find along the way — ooh, the mystery!

It all makes for a pretty appealing bundle, all things considered. We had a perfectly pleasant time with Splintered Fate back in 2024 (Hades comparisons aside, that is), calling it "a fun and approachable 'Hades-like' that's best played with pals" in our review. The DLC brings some welcome extras to really beef the game up, so if you've been eying the expansions for a while or if you've just been looking for an excuse to dive in, this may well be it!

Will you be checking out this Gold Edition? Let us know in the comments.

Jim came to Nintendo Life in 2022 and, despite his insistence that The Minish Cap is the best Zelda game and his unwavering love for the Star Wars prequels (yes, really), he has continued to write news and features on the site ever since.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/03/tmnt-splintered-fate-gold-edition-gets-totally-tubular-on-switch-today
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: PS5, PS5 Pro, PS Portal Consoles Might Be About to Get Substantially More Expensive
item.content:encoded:
• 0-20%: Unlikely - Lacks credible sources
  • 21-40%: Questionable - Some concerns remain
  • 41-60%: Plausible - Reasonable evidence
  • 61-80%: Probable - Strong evidence
  • 81-100%: Highly Likely - Multiple reliable sources

According to French content creator Gyo, retailers have been alerted to an imminent price hike across Sony's entire PlayStation console family, including PS5 Slim, PS5 Pro, and PS Portal. The prices of both the Slim and the Pro would increase by €100, with a higher percentage increase for the former, while the handheld cloud-powered PS Portal would be priced €30 higher than the current price.

Here's a table comparing the current and rumored pricing:

The content creator did not mention the PS5 Slim Digital Edition specifically, but if it follows the same +€100 pattern as the other increases, it could be priced at €599.99 with a steep 20% increase.

As you might recall, during its nearly six years on the market, the PlayStation 5 has already received two price hikes. The first one was announced on August 25, 2022, with a €50 increase on both the disc and digital editions of the consoles:

  • PS5 Disc Edition: €499.99 → €549.99 (+€50, +10%)
  • PS5 Digital Edition: €399.99 → €449.99 (+€50, +12.5%).

Then, on April 13, 2025, the PS5 Slim Digital Edition received another €50 increase, bringing the price to €499.99, a +11.1% price hike.

Take the rumor with a grain of salt, but given the current international climate and the ongoing memory and storage component crisis, another price hike for the PS5 is definitely not impossible. If confirmed, the PS5 Slim disc edition would cost more than Microsoft's Xbox Series X, which currently costs €599.99.

We'll stay on top of this rumor with any confirmations or denials from Sony. Stay tuned.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/ps5-ps5-pro-ps-portal-console-might-get-more-expensive/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: For better or worse, Max and Chloe are together again in Life is Strange: Reunion, which is out right now
item.content:encoded:
Reuinted and it feels so... complicated

A new Life is Strange game doesn't always feel particularly odd given its sort of steady turn into a franchise. The last one was only in 2024 with Life Is Strange: Double Exposure, bringing back the original game's Max Caulfield. This wasn't necessarily a welcome return for all, and ended up being somewhat of a mixed bag. And now we come to now with a potentially odd entry, Life is Strange: Reunion, which is out today. As the subtitle suggests, and where the oddness comes in, Reunion reunites Max with the surprising return of Chloe Price. Big spoilers ahead for Double Exposure, by the way!

For those of you who don't give a hoot about spoilers that are wondering how Max and Chloe can be in the same timeline, you have Double Exposure to thank for that. At the end of that game, Max fuses the separate timelines together, allowing Chloe to return in this game. In Reunion, Max is now working as a photography teacher at Caledon University, but, uh oh! After a cheeky little weekend holiday, she returns to discover the university on fire, killing all her friends. And so once again she rewinds time, finding Chloe in her life once again, who needs some help.

The time rewind mechanic is at the centre of things like in the original game, with Max having to undo and redo various decisions you make on behalf of her to solve puzzles. As Chloe, you can Backtalk, which is not some magical power, it's just talking cleverly or sarcastically. Better than nothing!

I'm sure that Reunion, much like Double Exposure before it, will be a tad divisive too. The launch trailer certainly suggests that Max and Chloe will be working to stick together this time around, but that feels like it undercuts the entire point of the original game's ending. I worry it leans a tad too much towards the fanservice side of the spectrum. That's not for me to decide, however! I shall leave that to experts.

You can pick up Life is Strange: Reunion on Steam right here and now.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/for-better-or-worse-max-and-chloe-are-together-again-in-life-is-strange-reunion-which-is-out-right-now
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: STALKER 2 Cost of Hope Expansion Revealed by GSC Game World
item.content:encoded:
GSC Game World has unveiled the first major story expansion for STALKER 2: Heart of Chornobyl, which was released in November 2024. The expansion, dubbed Cost of Hope, promises ‘dozens of hours of gameplay’, opens up new areas of The Zone, gives players new weapons and ways to fight, and casts the player into a brutal faction-driven war.

While STALKER 2 has received a few chunky updates over the last year and a half, they’ll pale in comparison to Cost of Hope, an absolute overhaul that impressively furthers the story and universe of Heart of Chornobyl.

When is Cost of Hope Coming Out?

GSC Game World took to the (digital) stage at the recent Xbox Partner Showcase to unveil Cost of Hope, the first major story expansion for STALKER 2: Heart of Chornobyl. In a press release, the team revealed what players are set to uncover in Cost of Hope:

Step into a fresh expedition across the Zone — a massive nonlinear expansion packed with dozens of hours of gameplay. Expect haunting stories, twisted characters, and that same heavy atmosphere of decay… with just a flicker of hope breaking through.

In Cost of Hope, Skif is front and centre once more, leaping into a new storyline that’s shaped by the player’s choices, just like in the base game. Except, this time, the ramifications of how STALKER fans choose to play can radiate far beyond The Zone.

This expansion is set to offer two new regions, the most important of which is the iconic Chornobyl Nuclear Power Plant, a terrifying, trepidous location fraught with danger and vicious mutants.

To fight those mutants (the array of which includes new threats), Skif will be equipped with new weapons and gear, including the legendary SKS carbine, which eagle-eyed fans will see a Duty soldier wielding in the trailer for the expansion:

GSC Game World explained that Cost of Hope is the ‘middle chapter of the second trilogy’, and it’ll be joined later by another story DLC. As the team said, ‘it will form a new, expansive narrative arc within the STALKER saga.’

Cost of Hope will be released in the summer on Xbox, PlayStation 5, and PC.

Are you looking forward to STALKER 2’s first expansion? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that three French studios have filed for insolvency

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/stalker-2-cost-of-hope-expansion-revealed-gsc/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Bluey Is Getting Another New Video Game This Year
item.content:encoded:
Bluey is coming back to the world of video games. Announced during the Xbox Partner Preview event on Thursday, Bluey’s Happy Snaps is an all-new experience launching in Fall 2026.

This isn’t the first foray into video games, as there have been a number of mobile offerings and crossovers with games like Minecraft. The first console game, Bluey: The Video Game, launched in 2023. The other game, Bluey’s Quest for the Gold Pen, is also in development and scheduled to launch in 2026.

Back to Bluey’s Happy Snaps, this game sees you adventure and explore the neighborhood, color, collect stickers, and more.

When it launches, the game will be available on PC and all consoles.

Are you excited to join the Heelers in Bluey’s Happy Snaps later this year? Leave your thoughts in the comments below, and in this official Insider Gaming Discord.

And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

MOBILE APP

During the Xbox Partner Preview, developer Panic Stations announced that Frog Sqwad is coming to Xbox platforms this summer.

Dispatch Coming to Xbox This Summer

Dispatch will be available on all consoles after releases on Xbox this summer. The game was announced for Xbox during the partner preview event on March 26.

Serious Sam: Shatterverse Revealed, Launches 2026

Serious Sam: Shatterverse has been officially revealed at this year’s Xbox Partner Preview, with the game announced to launch later this year.

item.title: Alien Deathstorm by Rebellion Games Revealed at Xbox Partner Preview
item.content:encoded:
Alien Deathstorm by Rebellion Games has been revealed during this year’s Xbox Partner preview.

You can watch the official reveal trailer of the game below:

Explore a devastated off-world colony that is ravaged by a cataclysmic mega-storm and overrun by bloodthirsty aliens in Alien Deathstorm, the new sci-fi action horror FPS from Rebellion. Using a combination of cunning and firepower you must defeat the countless lurking horrors in a desperate fight for survival.

Alien Deathstorm is coming to Xbox Series X|S and Xbox on PC as an Xbox Play Anywhere title and with Xbox Game Pass.

You can read more about the Xbox Partner Preview and what was announced here.

Are you excited for Alien Deathstorm? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that PS3 consoles just got an update

Tom Henderson is Insider Gaming's Co-Founder and Editor-In-Chief. When he's not running one of the industry's leading independent video game websites, he's probably playing an FPS like Call of Duty,…

More from Tom Henderson

Serious Sam: Shatterverse Revealed, Launches 2026

item.title: Dispatch Coming to Xbox This Summer
item.content:encoded:
Dispatch will be available on all consoles after releases on Xbox this summer. The game was announced for Xbox during the partner preview event on March 26.

The interactive visual novel was released in the latter half of 2025 on PC and PlayStation 5, and later on Nintendo Switch 2.

The Switch 2 version was controversial due to the game’s automatic censorship. The Xbox version of the game will likely not have this issue, and censorship will be up to the player. AdHoc later said that they were working with Nintendo to find a pathway forward around the censorship.

Dispatch Was Very Critically Acclaimed

Dispatch, along with Clair Obscur: Expedition 33, had the most nominations at the BAFTA Awards in 2026. The game was also heavily praised by the director of The Witcher 3. Konrad Tomaszkiewicz had labeled Dispatch as their Game of the Year. The game sold very well, selling 2 million copies in less than a month.

The game will be released on Xbox and be featured on Xbox Game Pass this Summer. Still, there’s no concrete release date.

Check out everything being announced at the current Xbox Partner Preview Event here.

Are you excited for the release of Dispatch on Game Pass? Were you waiting for it to be free-to-play before picking it up? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

item.title: Serious Sam: Shatterverse Revealed, Launches 2026
item.content:encoded:
Serious Sam: Shatterverse has been officially revealed at this year’s Xbox Partner Preview, with the game announced to launch later this year.

You can watch the full announcement trailer below:

Serious Sam: Shatterverse is a co-op roguelite FPS for 1 – 5 players. Blast through shifting universes, stack wild boons, bend the rules with run modifiers, and hunt Mental’s deadliest forces. Fall fast. Come back louder.

Coming to Xbox Series X|S and Xbox on PC as an Xbox Play Anywhere title.

You can read more about the Xbox Partner Preview and what was announced here.

Are you excited to play Serious Sam: Shatterverse? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that PS3 consoles just got an update

item.title: Star Wars Zero Company Gets New Screenshots
item.content:encoded:
A bunch of new Star Wars Zero Company screenshots have been revealed, showcasing different aspects of the game.

Players were recently treated to several announcements about Star Wars games at The Game Awards, with Star Wars: Galactic Racer and Star Wars: Fate of the Old Republic revealed. We have finally received information about another highly anticipated Star Wars title, Zero Company.

Star Wars Zero Company Screenshots Showcase Gameplay, Cutscenes, Menus, and More

The screenshots for Star Wars Zero Company were revealed through the latest issue of PC Gamer Magazine (via bespinbulletin). The first set of screenshots showcases gameplay, revealing the different types of enemies, UI, weapons, and more that players will use in the game.

Then players can see the galaxy map, with details on missions and enemies visible. Players also get to see a cutscene, the squad selection menu, character customisation, and the Krabb Uppercut through the screenshots.

For those unaware of the game, Star Wars Zero Company is a single-player, turn-based tactics game that has players commanding an elite squad through an authentic story. The game is set in the twilight of the Clone Wars, where you play as Haws, a former Republic officer who leads Zero Company.

In other news, Fortnite Creative players can begin making Star Wars-inspired islands from May 2026. Additionally, Star Wars: The Old Republic Director Casey Hudson has hit back at the rumors and speculation that his game will not release until at least 2030.

What are your thoughts on the new Star Wars Zero Company screenshots? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about God of War live-action series casting ‘Lost’ actor as Freya. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/star-wars-zero-company-gets-new-screenshots/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Road trip adventure game Forever Ago launches this fall for PS5, Xbox Series, Switch 2, and PC
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Switch 2
  • Xbox Series

Navigates the aftermath of a personal tragedy.

Third-person single-player road trip adventure game Forever Ago, which was first announced in July 2020, will launch for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, Switch 2, and PC via Steam, Epic Games Store, and Microsoft Store this fall, publisher Annapurna Interactive and developer Third Shift announced.

Here is an overview of the game, via Annapurna Interactive:

In Forever Ago, the player takes on the role of Alfred as he navigates the aftermath of a tragedy in his life. Propelled by his minivan and armed with an instant camera, he embarks on a journey north in search of redemption.

Along the way, Alfred will explore beautiful environments and meet fascinating new people while visiting beautiful and unique places, from serene forest trails to deserts to mountains, examining objects to help him understand his surroundings and the people who came before. He makes new memories and captures them with his instant camera, used to document his journey and solve small environmental puzzles.

Featuring an original soundtrack by Clark Aboud (Slay the Spire), Forever Ago is an emotional exploration of love, loss, and redemption.

Watch a new trailer below. View a new set of screenshots at the gallery.

Release Window Trailer

Screenshots

  • ❮
  • ❯


https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/road-trip-adventure-game-forever-ago-launches-this-fall-for-ps5-xbox-series-switch-2-and-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The Expanse: Osiris Reborn launches in spring 2027
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Xbox Series

New gameplay trailer released.

Third-person action RPG The Expanse: Osiris Reborn will launch for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam, Epic Games Store, and GOG in spring 2027, developer Owlcat Games announced.

Here is an overview of the game, via Owlcat Games:

About

The Expanse: Osiris Reborn is a third-person action RPG set in The Expanse universe. You’re no hero—just a merc caught in the wrong place at the wrong time, trying to hold your crew together and keep the ship up and running. Your choices will shape your story.

The Universe Never Told Us if We Did Right or Wrong

By the 24th century, humanity has conquered the cosmos. However, life has remained anything but easy. In The Expanse: Osiris Reborn, a story-driven sci-fi Action RPG, you play as a Pinkwater Security mercenary whose brief shore leave on the asteroid Eros spirals out of control. Trapped in a deadly lockdown and drawn into a solar system-wide conspiracy far beyond your control, you’ll have to adapt, survive, taking command of the most advanced ship in the whole of the solar system.

Create and customize your own captain—Earther, Martian, or Belter—and lead a crew of highly-skilled specialists as you face off against escalating threats, political pressure, and a relentless enemy that will stop at nothing to see you dead. In a solar system held together by fragile alliances, every decision leaves its mark.

Trust Isn’t Given—It’s Earned

Your companions are more than just mission assets—they are people with their own scars and loyalties. Some chase redemption. Others hide their truths. Over time, your relationships will flourish or deteriorate depending on the choices you make and how you choose to lead. The ship becomes your home—a place where trust is earned, not given.

Dynamic Third-Person Combat

Fight in third-person utilizing cover-focused gunplay and abilities. Two companions will accompany you into the field while others will support from a distance: disabling systems or drawing the enemies’ attention. You will issue real-time commands and have to adapt quickly to survive the manifold threats the system will throw at you.

The Fractured World of The Expanse

Visit iconic locations like Ganymede, Ceres, Mars, and Luna. Walk through ruined habitats and political strongholds mired in unease and tension. Talk to the locals. Uncover secrets. Use your skills to influence what happens next. The system looks different from every planet and from every asteroid. Some are falling apart. Others just hide it better. You may even come across a familiar face or voice that reminds you of just how small the system really is.

Watch a new trailer below.

Gameplay Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/the-expanse-osiris-reborn-launches-in-spring-2027
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Hunter: The Reckoning – Deathwish announced for PS5, Xbox Series, and PC
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Xbox Series

Due out in summer 2027.

Publisher Nacon and developer Teyon have announced Hunter: The Reckoning – Deathwish for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC (Steam, Microsoft Store). It will launch in summer 2027. It will also support Xbox Play Anywhere.

Here is an overview of the game, via Nacon:

Vampires, Werewolves, Witches. What if I told you that the things that go bump in the night are real? What if I told you that you could see them?

Awaken to the truth you’re now a Hunter. Build your character, select skills, and work with a team of allies to investigate the monsters hidden in plain sight, to find their lairs, and to eradicate them. All the while, investigating a story that drags up memories from your past, ones that could change how you see the future.

Welcome to the World of Darkness.

Watch the announcement trailer below.

Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/hunter-the-reckoning-deathwish-announced-for-ps5-xbox-series-and-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Pocket Mirror: GoldenerTraum coming to PS5, PS4
item.content:encoded:
• PS4
  • PS5

Currently available for PC.

Publisher KOMODO and developers AstralShift and VisuStella will release horror RPG Pocket Mirror: GoldenerTraum for PlayStation 5 and PlayStation 4, the companies announced. A release date was not announced.

Pocket Mirror: GoldenerTraum first launched for PC via Steam on May 19, 2023.

Here is an overview of the game, via KOMODO:

About

Welcome to a world of wonder and darkness, where reality and dreams intertwine and secrets lurk around every corner.

Pocket Mirror is a surreal indie adventure game that will take you on a journey through a mysterious realm where nothing is what it seems and everything is connected.

As a young girl with a clouded past, you wake up in a strange and eerie mansion with nothing but your pocket mirror in hand. In search of your memories, you must explore the twisted corridors and rooms of the mansion, solve puzzles, interact with unique characters, and uncover the truth behind your identity and the sinister forces that are at play.

Will you be able to face the truth?

With its hauntingly beautiful art style, mesmerizing soundtrack, and captivating storyline, Pocket Mirror is a unique and unforgettable gaming experience that will challenge your mind, touch your heart, and leave you breathless.

Key Features

  • A surreal and atmospheric world full of mysteries and wonders to discover.
  • Intriguing puzzles and challenges that will test your skills and logic.
  • A cast of memorable and complex characters with multiple paths and endings, depending on your choices and actions.
  • A hauntingly beautiful soundtrack that will immerse you in the game’s atmosphere.
  • A rich and deep lore that rewards exploration and curiosity.
  • An unforgettable journey of self-discovery, loss, and hope.

You shouldn’t ever let it go, do you understand?

Watch a new trailer below.

PlayStation Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/pocket-mirror-goldenertraum-coming-to-ps5-ps4
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Stranger Than Heaven Will Take Place Across Five Time Periods, Deep-Dive Presentation Arriving This May
item.content:encoded:
Like a Dragon/Yakuza developer RGG Studio has released a new trailer for its upcoming game, Stranger Than Heaven, which reveals that its narrative will unfold across five different time periods. Plus, RGG has announced that a deep-dive presentation for the game is coming in May. 

Stranger Than Heaven was first announced under the working title Project Century at The Game Awards 2024, revealing a jazz-infused action game set across different historical periods. A year later, at The Game Awards 2025, RGG released a new trailer for the game, revealing the official "Stranger Than Heaven" name in the process. We still don't know when to expect Stranger Than Heaven, but today's new trailer, which aired during an Xbox Partner Preview showcase, reveals that its story spans different eras of history: 1915, 1929, 1943, 1951, and 1965. 

Check it out for yourself in the latest Stranger Than Heaven trailer below: 

Alongside this new trailer, Xbox and RGG have revealed they will hold an "Xbox Presents: A Special Look At Stranger Than Heaven" presentation on May 6, promising a deep-dive "into the setting, story, characters, and much more." 

For more details about the game, head to this Xbox Wire blog post. 

Are you excited by Stranger Than Heaven's latest showing? Let us know in the comments below!



https://gameinformer.com/xbox-partner-preview/2026/03/26/stranger-than-heaven-will-take-place-across-five-time-periods-deep
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Dead By Daylight Devs Announce Serious Sam: Shatterverse, A Multiverse, Multiplayer Roguelite Shooter
item.content:encoded:
Serious Sam is making his return, and this time, he's not alone. Revealed during today's Xbox Partner Preview, Serious Sam: Shatterverse is a Spiderverse-inspired take on the first-person shooter, bringing Sams from several dimensions into the same adventure. You can check out the reveal trailer below.

Shatterverse features five playable Sams, each with distinct art styles. Since the game is multiplayer, you and up to four friends can play at once, each assuming the role of a different Sam. Classic weapons will return, but they'll exist alongside new "all-new toys" from the Shatterverse. While previous games in the series were helmed by Croatian studio Croteam, Shatterverse is being developed by Dead by Daylight developer Behaviour Interactive, with Devolver Digital returning to publish. 

“Our main goal was to respect Serious Sam's rich legacy while presenting it in a fresh, contemporary way unlike anything seen before in the series,” Behaviour Interactive’s Nic Duchesne wrote in a press release. “Working with Devolver has been an incredible, enriching experience for our team. The folks there are real renegades of the video game publishing world: they always put the game first and aren’t afraid to carve their own path.”

While the game was announced during the Xbox Partner Preview, it's not exclusive to the platform – Serious Sam: Shatterverse launches on PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X/S, and PC later this year.



https://gameinformer.com/xbox-partner-preview/2026/03/26/dead-by-daylight-devs-announce-serious-sam-shatterverse-a
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Hunter: The Reckoning – Deathwish Is A First-Person Action Game Set In The 'World Of Darkness' Universe Arriving Summer 2027
item.content:encoded:
Hunter: The Reckoning – Deathwish is a first-person action game set in the World of Darkness universe, which also includes franchises like Vampire: The Masquerade. Microsoft opened today's Xbox Partner Preview with this announcement, revealing Deathwish will hit Xbox Series X/S, Xbox on PC, and Xbox Cloud in the Summer of 2027. 

This first trailer follows a team of hunters out to take down a dangerous vampire masquerading (wink wink) as a police officer while also teasing some of the other locations you'll explore in the game. If you've been gaming for a minute, you might recognize Hunter: The Reckoning, as it was a hack-and-slash/third-person shooter that launched on the original Xbox in 2002 and the Nintendo GameCube the following year. 

Check out the Hunter: The Reckoning – Deathwish reveal trailer for yourself below: 

You can read more about the game at the Xbox Wire blog post here. 

Deathwish follows last year's Vampire: The Masquerade – Bloodlines 2, the most recent game in the World of Darkness universe, and though they occupy the same world, it remains unclear if they're connected at all. You can read Game Informer's Vampire: The Masquerade – Bloodlines 2 review here, and then check out our Vampire: The Masquerade – Bloodlines 2 hub for more behind-the-scenes information about the World of Darkness from our cover story last year. 

Did you play the original Hunter: The Reckoning? Let us know in the comments below!



https://gameinformer.com/xbox-partner-preview/2026/03/26/hunter-the-reckoning-deathwish-is-a-first-person-action-game-set-in
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Serious Sam: Shatterverse revealed in Xbox Partner Showcase, and it's being made by the Dead by Daylight devs
item.content:encoded:
Serious Sam is back! Announced during today's Xbox Partner Showcase, Serious Sam: Shatterverse is a co-operative multiplayer Roguelite FPS from Devolver Digital and Behavior Interactive. It's set to release some time this year on Xbox Series X/S, PS5, and PC.

Serious Sam: Shatterverse - coming from the creators of Dead by Daylight - is a celebratory romp through 25 years of Serious Sam. Through a shattered universes narrative, different Sam's from multiple dimensions can team up and blast through enemies new and old. Through the Roguelite twist, you'll be able to stack up different boons to mix up the gameplay.

You can watch the new trailer for Serious Sam: Shatterverse below!

Like prior, perhaps more traditional Serious Sam games, you'll have a vast arsenal of weapons to use to your heart's content, and massive hordes of enemies as you'd expect given the series' heritage. This will be mixed with new ideas, such as procedural shifted runs and unstable anomalies which shake up the gameplay.

While notably not being handled by the original Serious Sam developers at Croteam, the co-creator of Serious Sam has provided his blessing alongside the company as a whole.

He provided the following statement alongside the Shatterverse reveal: "Seeing Shatterverse come to life is a really big moment for us. It's very exciting to see another talented team step in and create something bold, fresh, and different within the universe we cherished for so long. I can't wait for both the old and new fans to experience this new interpretation of the series!"

Behavior Interactive has had itself a busy month, having only recently bought up 7 Days to Die in an out-of-nowhere deal. It's crown jewel release - Dead by Daylight - retains its momentum with a number of exciting releases coming out later this year.

Related topics


https://www.eurogamer.net/serious-sam-shatterverse-revealed-in-xbox-partner-showcase-a-new-co-op-roguelite-fps
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Xbox Partner Preview showcase March 2026 - here's everything announced
item.content:encoded:
What better way to celebrate a Thursday than another Xbox Partner Preview showcase? Yes, it’s that time again when Microsoft turns to its chums and offers them the spotlight; a chance to show the world what they've been working on. As usually, that likely means a mix of bigger third-party games and some smaller, but no less enticing indie treats. We’ll be charting the news as it arrives, so ready yourself for the reveals below.

Hunter: The Reckoning - Deathwish

First up we’ve had a brand-new game in the Hunter: The Reckoning series. It’s been over two decades since the tabletop RPG series got a video game adaptation, but here we are weih Hunter: The Reckoning - Deathwish, which is being published by Nacon and is heading to Xbox Series X/S, PS5, and PC in summer 2027.

Wuthering Waves is coming to Xbox

Need more wuthering in your life? Well you’re luck's in, because developer Kuro Games’ free-to-play, open-world action-RPG Wuthering Waves is finally making its way to Xbox following its earlier PlayStation 5 and PC release. If you’re in the mood for a bit of gacha, keep your eyes trained on Xbox Series X/S this July, which is when the double-doubleyoo is scheduled to arrive.

The Expanse: Osiris Reborn gets a release window and beta

Perhaps you've already have heard of developer Owlcat's promising The Expanse: Osiris Reborn, a Mass Effect-style take on the popular sci-fi TV show and novel series. Well, if your interest is piqued, we now know it’s launching in spring 2027, and impatient sorts can get involved even earlier, in a closed beta demo releasing next month - but you’ll need to have pre-purchased one of the more expensive editions to play it. Bit of a rollercoaster announcement, then, but at least you can look at the new trailer for free.

Related topics


https://www.eurogamer.net/xbox-partner-preview-showcase-march-2026-heres-everything-announced
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Owlcat's Mass Effecty sci-fi game The Expanse: Osiris Reborn gets a release window, a closed beta date, and a Game Pass promise
item.content:encoded:
Owlcat's promising Mass Effecty take on sci-fi fiction The Expanse - Expanse: Osiris Reborn - has been given a release window of next spring (2027), but there will be a closed beta demo released next month so people who've pre-purchased the more expensive editions of the game can try it.

Coinciding with the announcement are a short gameplay trailer showing the beta content, and word that Expanse: Osiris Reborn will be a day-one Game Pass Ultimate game when it arrives.

The closed beta will be available from 22nd April on all platforms the game is coming to: PC, PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series S/X, and you'll be able to access it if you've bought the Collector's Edition of The Expanse: Osiris Reborn ($289!) or the Miller's Pack version of the game ($80).

In terms of content, the closed beta lets you access one mission relatively early in the game - it's the second mission in the game, I was told in a press briefing - just after your protagonist and your sibling have escaped a catastrophe on a planet called Eros and snagged yourselves a ship. You'll be able to choose from a couple of backgrounds, a couple of classes, and experience a cross-section of the experiences The Expanse: Osiris Reborn offers.

So, things like cover-based group combat - think Mass Effect, complete with companion orders - cinematic dialogue with choices (think Mass effect again) and a bit of equipment upgrading and levelling up. It's all very Mass Effect, albeit with less space magic and more believable sci-fi gadgetry. And you'll be able to explore inside a space station during the demo and outside of it, clomping around in mag-boots in zero-gravity so you don't accidentally float off into space, wee!

The idea of a closed beta for a single-player game strikes me as slightly odd, as there's no multiplayer component to test, but apparently this is something Owlcat does regularly. "We still want to check how the game will be received by the players - see feedback - and we still have time to iterate some systems based on feedback," design director Leonid Rastorguev told me. "So we really want our players to play the game and give us their opinions."

Game design producer Yuliya Chernenko added: "We're used to doing such closed beta tests, or something very similar, throughout any game development. Even our other games also had something very similar. So this is something for us to showcase our game, to get feedback, to understand how people perceive this game - whether they like it or not."

Owlcat used a similar approach making its two Pathfinder games (Kingmaker and Wrath of the Righteous), and for Warhammer 40K: Rogue Trader - the computer role-playing games the Cyprus-based studio built a reputation on. We didn't review the Pathfinder games but did review Rogue Trader, awarding it four stars. "Rogue Trader nails the 40k setting and provides an appropriately massive narrative filled with meaty tactical combat, though some bugs and poor performance hold it back," we wrote in our Warhammer 40K: Rogue Trader review.

As a third-person, Unreal Engine-built, over-the-shoulder action RPG, The Expanse: Osiris Reborn represents a new and ambitious push for the studio, and I'm surprised to discover there are 200 people working on it. There are also a further 60-70 people working on a Rogue Trader follow-up called Warhammer 40K: Dark Heresy. There's lots of promise, although there are concerns surrounding Owlcat and its use of gen-AI during development of The Expanse: Osiris Reborn. Nevertheless, I hope to bring you fuller thoughts on the closed beta when I can.

The Expanse: Osiris Reborn is not to be confused with that other Mass Effecty-looking sci-fi game Exodus, which is also, coincidentaly, being released in 2027.

Related topics


https://www.eurogamer.net/owlcats-expanse-osiris-reborn-release-window-beta-date
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Japanese Charts: Pokémon Pokopia Shows No Signs Of Slowing Down
item.content:encoded:
It's time for the weekly physical Japanese charts, published by Famitsu. And for the third week in a row, Pokémon Pokopia sits proudly at the top.

It isn't exactly close, either — the fantastic block-based cosy adventure sold another 114,515 boxed copies during the week of 16th March to 22nd March 2026. Second place went to the only new release in the top ten, the huge open-world action game Crimson Desert, which sold 31,108 copies.

Otherwise, besides Resident Evil Requiem's PS5 release in 6th, it's all Switch and Switch 2. Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection, Mario Kart World and Mario Tennis Fever round out the top five, while Animal Crossing: New Horizon occupies two spots with the old Switch 1 release and the Nintendo Switch 2 Edition.

Pokémon FireRed & LeafGreen's Download Card release has crept up to the top ten as well, selling 5,872 copies this week.

So, as you'll see below, outside of Pokopia and Crimson Desert, it's been a relatively quiet week. Will Pokopia stay on-top next week? We'll see, but for now, here's this week's top ten in full:

Position Game Platform Unit Sales (16th - 22nd Mar) Total Unit Sales
1

Pokémon Pokopia

Switch 2

 114,515 772,915
2

Crimson Desert

NEW
3

Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection

4

8,276

 2,873,338
5

7,833

90,975
6

Resident Evil Requiem

 6,652
 198,271
7

Pokémon FireRed & LeafGreen (Download Card)

Switch

 5,872
 5,932
8

Animal Crossing: New Horizons

9

Minecraft

Switch

 4,451

4,173,164
10

Animal Crossing: New Horizons - Nintendo Switch 2 Edition

Switch 2

 3,784

91,914

On the console side of things, it's a similar story: the Switch 2 is again the best-selling hardware of the week, selling another 81,518 units and fast approaching 5 million sales.

The three Switch 1 SKUs have sold a combined 18,751 units, compared to the PS5's own trio, which shifted 14,119 — though the Digital Edition was the second best-selling console of the week.

For a breakdown of all consoles, here's the hardware charts in full:

Position Console
 Unit Sales (16th - 22nd Mar)

What are your thoughts on this week's Japanese charts? Share them in the usual spot below.

[source famitsu.com]

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: PSSR 2 Provides “Overall a Much Better Image” Across Multiple Games on PS5 Pro, But There Are Some Remaing Issues, Says Digital Foundry
item.content:encoded:
PlayStation's initial foray into developing its own upscaling technology with PSSR (PlayStation Spectral Super Resolution) was, unfortunately for PlayStation, very hit and miss. It's never a good thing when the new technology that is supposed to make games look better, on the latest and greatest hardware, look worse than it does without that tech on the old hardware. Thankfully, though, it seems like PSSR 2, the upgraded and enhanced version of the upscaler is capable of delivering "overall, a much better image," across multiple titles, according to Digital Foundry's Oliver Mackenzie. Despite it still having some remaining issues.

That comes from the outlet's latest test on over 20 titles, each of which supports PSSR 2 in one of three ways. Firstly, and perhaps the most obvious one, are titles that get specific PSSR 2 patches from their developers. Secondly, and a little less obvious though just as convenient for users, are the games that have been whitelisted by developers. In this instance, it's not a direct patch to the game that enables PSSR 2, but your PS5 Pro's latest software update is forcing the new upscaler over PSSR 1.

Third, and the one that players not in-the-know could miss, is by manually enabling the 'Enhance PSSR Image Quality' setting in your console's visual settings. If you want to use PSSR 2, you'll need to do this even for games that already support PSSR 1. Because if they haven't been patched to use PSSR 2 by default, and they are not one of the games (of which there are currently only 11) that have been whitelisted, you'll be stuck on PSSR 1 if you don't enable that setting.

Digital Foundry tested a little over 20 titles for this report, and while the outlet still sees issues in certain games like Dragon's Dogma 2 and Star Wars Jedi: Survivor, in other cases, like Gran Turismo 7, it's clear how improved PSSR 2 is over its predecessor, delivering "greater stability, especially in motion," according to Mackenzie.

PSSR 2 is also able to deliver "minimal noise" across multiple titles, specifically those built on Unreal Engine 5, as RTGI noise was a significant issue for titles built on UE5 using PSSR 1. Games like Silent Hill f look "completely different" for the better thanks to this improvement.

But as previously stated, there are remaining issues. The aforementioned Star Wars Jedi: Survivor gets called out for its ray-traced reflections looking "sharp and noisy in motion," and both Jedi Survivor and Dragon's Dogma 2 feature "dot patterns" that, once again, make things look off in a noticeable way.

Overall though, at least for DF's testing, PSSR 2 is far and away the upscaler to use when playing any of the supported titles on your PS5 Pro. It's definitely an improvement, and it seems lik PlayStation is taking steps in the right direction as it continues down the path of developing its own upscaler technology.

But all of that doesn't solve the real problem PlayStation has when trying to sell PSSR 2, a PS5 Pro, and eventually, the PlayStation 6, to players, according to Alderon Games' founder.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/pssr-2-overall-a-much-better-image-there-are-remaining-issues-digital-foundry/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Hytale Update 4 Brings Over 500 New Blocks and More
item.content:encoded:
Hytale has received a brand new update, and this one's got hundreds of new blocks for you to experiment with, plus a new proximity chat feature and more.

As revealed in the official patch notes for Update 4 (catchy name, right?), "around 500" new blocks have been introduced as part of this latest patch, including sets for materials like limestone, chalk, and slate.

If you're socially-minded while playing Hytale, you'll be pleased to know a new proximity voice chat feature has been added, allowing you to enjoy the game's blocky sandbox world alongside others more easily. Communication has never been so easy (in Hytale, that is; pre-existing communication issues may still apply).

Voice chat is, naturally, disabled by default, and you can't enable it for younger users. You can also look forward to different audio effects based on where you are in Hytale's world, which is a nice touch.

As well as proximity chat, a new emote wheel has been added, so you should have a range of new ways to express yourself even if you don't feel like chatting with your fellow players.

Seven new hairstyles have also been added as part of Update 4, along with a brand new creative tool that lets you "paste rotations of your current selection any number of times or fully around a center point".

In case you need context, Hytale is a massively ambitious sandbox RPG that's been through the wringer a few times. After being acquired by Riot in 2020, the studio subsequently canceled the project in 2025 before it was revived by developer Hypixel's original founders.

The game was subsequently released in Early Access in January this year, and following its release, it was able to recoup enough to cover two years of its development, so it's safe to say this one's been a success story (eventually).

You can check out Hytale right now on PC, where it's currently enjoying an extended Early Access period. Stay tuned for more.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/hytale-update-4-new-blocks
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Saros director won't say whether it's coming to PC, following report Sony plan to stop porting over their big singleplayer guns
item.content:encoded:
Whoever chats to him next will have to Returnal to this line of questioning

I dunno if you heard earlier this month, but PlayStation are reportedly breaking up with us PC folks, at least when it comes to the PS5's biggest singleplayer hitters. Asked where Returnal devs Housemarque's new game Saros - due out next month - fits into that, its director has opted to remain tight-lipped.

"Today we’re only talking about Saros and the launch on the PlayStation 5," Gregory Louden said when asked about PC portage of the game by Game Informer. Were it still the halcyon days prior to that report Sony are looking not to bring the likes of Ghost of Yōtei to PC, we could have taken this non-answer in stride, assuming that a Saros PC port might well pop up in a couple of years' time.

That, after all, is what happened with Returnal back in 2023. Alas, no such things can be taken for granted now, with console makers reportedly so concerned about selling consoles once more that they feel compelled to keep hold of their prized possessions in the name of exclusivity. That obviously doesn't apply to multiplayer games like Bungie's Marathon, which benefit a lot more from being able to welcome a horde of PC players right out of the gate.

It's fair to say that, once he got his hands on its PC port for our Returnal review, Jon Bailes felt the wait had been worth it. "Keep playing and each turn reveals with increasing certainty that this is a game totally at one with itself, from its audio-visual spectacle to its pinpoint control to its interweaving narrative and now its longevity," he wrote. "Unlike Selene, I keep coming back to Atropos by choice. Long may its immaculate, horrifying loop continue."

Saros has looked to be heading down a less punishing, but equally bullet-filled road. Here's hoping it does meander its way across the great game box divide at some point.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/saros-director-wont-say-whether-its-coming-to-pc-following-report-sony-plan-to-stop-porting-over-their-big-singleplayer-guns
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: All Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 Week 2 Quests & How To Complete Them (March 26)
item.content:encoded:
Carrying on the Fortnite tradition this season is the addition of new, weekly challenges. For details on every Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 Week 2 Quest and the attached rewards, read on for a complete guide.

In the middle of a tense battle between the Ice King and the Foundation, you can pick off some weekly challenges to earn XP and improve your standing in Fortnite. We’ve already extensively covered the Week 1 Quests, but now it’s time for a fresh set of tasks.

Every Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 Week 2 Quest & Reward

A further five Quests have been added in Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2, and the Week 2 extras require you to defeat the Ice King, destroy objects, and take out other players.

  • More Insider Gaming: How To Get The Skyline Deployer in Fortnite

Check out every Week 2 challenge in our handy table below.

When Do Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 Week 3 Quests Release?

As before, the complete set of Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 Week 3 Quests will drop one week after the launch of Week 2’s Quests, giving you an April 2 release date.

The day after April Fool’s Day is ominous! However, this shouldn’t have any bearing on the next lot of Fortnite quests.

  • More Insider Gaming: How To Use The Jam Track Player in Fortnite

Please check back in with Insider Gaming as we run you through all Week 3 Quests once they’re up, including how to complete them, and possibly give you concentrated guides on completing individual quests if they’re tricky.

How do Week 2’s Quests compare to the previous challenges? Are you disappointed that there are only five? How can Epic keep quests fresh this season and in future seasonal content updates? Share your feedback and ideas via the Insider Gaming Discord.

We have many more guides and hubs for relevant Fortnite questions and hot topics:

  • When is Festival Battle Stage Being Removed From Fortnite?
  • When is Ballistic Being Removed From Fortnite?
  • When is Rocket Racing Being Removed From Fortnite?
  • Is Fortnite Shutting Down? Epic Games Layoffs, Explained

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Xbox Partner Preview March 2026—Everything Announced
item.content:encoded:
On Thursday afternoon, Xbox held its latest Partner Preview event for March 2026. With the show comes a look at a number of games with updates and new reveals.

Thursday’s event is the fifth Xbox Partner Preview showcase, with the last one happening in November 2025. That one featured games like Vampire Crawlers, 007 First Light, Reanimal, and CloverPit, among others.

The Xbox Partner Preview starts at 1pm ET/10am PT/5pm GMT on March 26, and airs on the official Xbox Twitch and YouTube Channels—you can also watch it below without having to leave the site. As far as the games, the show includes new looks at titles like STALKER 2, Stranger Than Heaven, The Expanse: Osiris Reborn, and more. There will also be announcements on games coming to Xbox Game Pass in the future.

As usual, Insider Gaming will have a full rundown of everything shown as they are announced. Since this is an updating article, make sure to bookmark and refresh often, starting at 1pm ET. And be sure to join the discussion in the comments and over in the official Insider Gaming Discord.

Everything Announced At Xbox Partner Preview March 2026

This article will begin updating at 1pm ET/10am PT/5pm GMT. Make sure to refresh often for the latest announcements from the Xbox Partner Preview event for March 2026.

Hunter: The Reckoning – Deathwish – Coming Summer 2027 (Full Story)

Wuthering Waves – Coming To Xbox

The Expanse: Osiris Reborn

Grave Seasons – Release Date

Serious Sam: Shatterverse (Full Story)

Stranger Than Heaven

Super Meat Boy 3D

Forever Ago

Ascend to Zero

Dispatch

Alien Deathstorm

In other gaming news, read about Microsoft being ranked higher than both Nintendo and Sony in the latest Metacritic publisher rankings. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/xbox-partner-preview-march-2026-everything-announced/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream Demo Turns All Your Miis Into Homebound Advertising Robots if You Buy Them Clothes
item.content:encoded:
Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream Demo Turns All Your Miis Into Homebound Advertising Robots if You Buy Them Clothes

I hear there's lots of things to do in the retail version.

Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream has a demo out now, and players are already using it to create absolutely ridiculous Miis and throw them into silly situations. But a word of caution if you're playing the demo yourself: don't play too much. If you get too far into the demo, eventually all your Miis will lock themselves in their houses and only robotically talk about buying the full version.

This extremely silly and apropos way of limiting a free demo has been discovered by multiple players already. Apparently, after you play the demo for a while, you are prompted to create a clothing store. If you make one for them, you'll be stuck on a pathway to the whole demo shutting down: you'll create a third Mii, build a clothing store, and then everyone will go into their houses, refuse to leave, and start telling you to buy the full version of the game.

There is a way around this: simply don't create that third Mii. Once you create that third Mii, you're set on a forced track to the end of the demo, even if you try to avoid building the clothing store afterward. But if you never make the third Mii, it seems you can keep playing the demo infinitely, though that does mean you never get to make more than two Miis or dress your Miis up until the full game comes out in April.

It's not really a shock that the demo has a cut-off point: it's a free demo, after all. If it let you play infinitely, it would just be the game. But it is a pretty funny way to stop people from playing more, and may come as unexpected to players unaware they're about to be shut down. It's also an appropriately silly way for a game like Tomodachi Life to end a demo - the fun of Tomodachi Life is in silly interactions and reactions from the characters, so everyone suddenly becoming a shut-in and trying to sell you on the full game just kind of works.

Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream just got a brand new extended gameplay look from Nintendo, which shows off the depth of Mii customization options, including the ability to just draw their faces freestyle (uh oh). That probably explains Nintendo's signficantly more strict image-sharing restrictions implemented for Living the Dream, which drew criticism when they were announced in January.

Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream will launch, and free your homebound Miis from their prison, on April 16.

Rebekah Valentine is a senior reporter for IGN. Got a story tip? Send it to rvalentine@ign.com.

Recommends

In This Article



https://www.ign.com/articles/tomodachi-life-living-the-dream-demo-turns-all-your-miis-into-homebound-advertising-robots-if-you-buy-them-clothes
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
